sections.paragraph:
Ἐλισάβετ, ἡ indecl. (אֱלִישֶׁבַע; s. EKönig, ET 21, 1910, 185–87; LKöhler, AKaegi Festschr. 1919, 186.—SEG VIII, 202 [I b.c./I a.d., ossuary fr. Jerus.] Ἐλισάβη) Elizabeth (cp. Ex 6:23; Philo, Poster. Cai. 76), wife of Zacharias the priest, mother of John the Baptist Lk 1:5, 7, 13, 24, 36, 40f, 57; GEb 13, 75; GJs 12:2 and 3; 22:3 (Just., D. 84, 4). According to a few witnesses also Lk 1:46 (on this s. Harnack, SBBerlAk 1900, 538ff=Studien I ’31, 62ff; Zahn, Lk: Exkurs III p. 745ff [older lit. here]; ENorden, D. Geburt d. Kindes 1924, 76ff; RHarris, ET 41, 1930, 266f; 42, ’31, 188–90). Acc. to vs. 36 and GJs 12:2 Mary, the mother of Jesus, was related to her.—UMittman-Richert, Magnifikat u. Benediktus ’96, 94–97. BHHW I 401.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

ET ET = Expository Times—List 6

Festschr. Festschr. = Festschrift, in ref. to any honorary publication

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

  1. fr. = from
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

GEb GEb = Gospel of the Ebionites—List 1

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 317
